2605.21076.title: GradeLegal: Automated Grading for German Legal Cases
2605.21076.paper_summary: Grading German legal exam solutions faces growing volumes and a shortage of qualified graders, delaying feedback and creating a bottleneck. At the same time, it is a high-stakes expert task, since state exam grades strongly influence career outcomes in Germany. Despite this practical relevance, literature lacks systematic studies on effective methods for grading legal exams. To address this gap, we investigate whether large language models (LLMs) can support the automated grading of German legal case solutions in criminal and public law, thereby enabling scalable feedback and student self-testing. We present a systematic evaluation of 27 proprietary and open-source LLMs, benchmarking prompting strategies that incrementally add task-related information, such as a sample solution and a grading rubric. Using quadratic weighted kappa (QWK), reasoning-oriented LLMs can approximate expert grading in public law when given a sample solution and a grading rubric (up to 0.91), compared to 0.60 in criminal law, suggesting a harder grading task in criminal law. Beyond single-model grading, ensembling improves agreement by up to 0.15 over its best member and can offer an alternative to stronger closed-source single models. In addition, our findings suggest that effective prompt design and model selection are necessary for reliable LLM-based grading of legal exams.
2605.21076.paper_summary_zh: 評分德國法律考試解答面臨著不斷增長的工作量和合格評分者的短缺，這導致了反饋的延遲並造成了瓶頸。與此同時，這是一項高風險的專家任務，因為國家考試成績對德國的職業結果有著強烈的影響。儘管這具有實際相關性，但文獻中缺乏對有效法律考試評分方法的系統研究。為了解決這一空白，我們調查大型語言模型（LLMs）是否可以支持德國刑法和公法的法律案例解答的自動評分，從而實現可擴展的反饋和學生自我測試。我們對27個專有和開源的LLMs進行了系統評估，基準測試逐步添加任務相關信息的提示策略，例如示例解答和評分標準。使用二次加權kappa（QWK），當提供示例解答和評分標準時，面向推理的LLMs在公法中的評分接近專家評分（高達0.91），而在刑法中則為0.60，這表明刑法的評分任務更具挑戰性。除了單模型評分外，集成方法能夠提高協議，最高可比其最佳成員提高0.15，並且可以作為更強的封閉源單模型的替代方案。此外，我們的研究結果表明，有效的提示設計和模型選擇對於可靠的基於LLM的法律考試評分是必要的。
2605.21076.author: Abdullah Al Zubaer et.al.
2605.21076.authors: Abdullah Al Zubaer, Lorenz Wendlinger, Simon Alexander Nonn, Michael Granitzer, Jelena Mitrovic
